title: Tell El-Maskhuta (Pithom)
description: The site of the ancient city of Pithom (Per-Atum), featuring ruins of massive storehouses, a temple dedicated to Atum, and significant Hyksos and Roman remains. It was a key city in the Wadi Tumilat trade route.

title: Tell El-Rataba
description: A significant fortress city from the Ramesside period, believed to correspond to the biblical Rameses for a time. It served as a strategic defense point and settlement in Wadi Tumilat.

title: Tell El-Sahaba
description: An archaeological tell located near Tell El-Maskhuta, yielding pottery and artifacts dating back to the earliest periods of Egyptian history and settlement in the wadi.

title: Tell El-Kua
description: Located in the Wadi Tumilat, this site has provided evidence of Hyksos settlement and integration with local Egyptian culture during the Second Intermediate Period.

title: Tell El-Ahmar
description: One of the reddish-colored mounds in the region, containing stratified remains of domestic and military structures from various ancient epochs.

title: Tell Hassan Dawood
description: A major Predynastic and Early Dynastic cemetery site. Excavations have revealed hundreds of tombs, including high-status burials with jewelry and stone vessels, indicating early social stratification.

title: Tell El-Dafana (Daphnae)
description: Identified as the biblical Tahpanhes and ancient Daphnae. A border fortress and settlement established by Psamtik I, known for its mercenary garrison and connections to the prophet Jeremiah.

title: Tell El-Gheita
description: An archaeological site in the western part of Wadi Tumilat, preserving remains of settlements that thrived on the trade and agricultural activities of the corridor.

title: De Lesseps House Museum
description: The private residence of Ferdinand de Lesseps during the construction of the Suez Canal. It houses his personal belongings, maps, diaries, and original furniture, offering a glimpse into the canal's history.

title: Old Suez Canal Authority Building (Al-Irshad)
description: The historic headquarters of the Suez Canal Company. An architectural marvel from the colonial era, it served as the administrative heart of the canal's operations.

title: Khedivial Rest House
description: Built for Khedive Ismail to oversee the canal's inauguration celebrations. It represents the lavish style of the era and the city's royal connections.

title: Ismailia Museum
description: Constructed in 1934 by the Suez Canal Company in the form of a Pharaonic pylon. It houses over 4,000 artifacts from Pharaonic, Greco-Roman, and Islamic eras discovered during the canal's excavation.

title: Abbasi Mosque
description: One of the oldest mosques in Ismailia, built by Khedive Abbas Helmy II. It features distinct Islamic architectural style and serves as a major religious landmark.

title: Church of St. Mark (Catholic)
description: A historic Catholic church built by the French community during the canal's construction. Known for its distinct Gothic revival architecture.

title: Orabi's Trenches
description: The site of the decisive Battle of Tel El-Kebir between the Egyptian army led by Ahmed Orabi and British forces. Remnants of earthworks and trenches can still be traced in the desert.

title: Commonwealth War Cemetery
description: A beautifully maintained cemetery containing the graves of soldiers who died in the region during the First and Second World Wars, reflecting the area's strategic military importance.

title: Tell El-Makhzan
description: Located near the canal, this site features remains of storage facilities and settlements that supported trade crossing between Africa and Asia.

title: Tell Abu-Seify (Sile/Tjaru)
description: The ancient fortress of Tjaru (Sile), the starting point of the Way of Horus. A massive fortress complex guarding Egypt's eastern border.

title: Tabet El-Shagara
description: A fortified Israeli strongpoint captured by the Egyptian army during the October War. It is preserved as a museum displaying bunkers, command centers, and tanks.

title: Monument of the Unknown Soldier
description: A distinctive monument located on Jebel Maryam overlooking the canal, commemorating the Egyptian sacrifices in the defense of Ismailia and the Canal.

title: French Hospital (Saint Vincent de Paul)
description: An architectural landmark established by the Suez Canal Company to provide medical care, reflecting the colonial French influence on the city's development.

title: Ferdinand de Lesseps Statue Base
description: The original base of the statue of Ferdinand de Lesseps. The statue was removed in 1956, but the site remains a historical marker of the canal's controversial history.
